messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"512\" height=\"512\" viewBox=\"0 0 512 512\">\
  <defs>\
    <radialGradient id=\"grass\" cx=\"50%\" cy=\"30%\" r=\"70%\">\
      <stop offset=\"0%\" stop-color=\"#4fd07a\"/>\
      <stop offset=\"100%\" stop-color=\"#1e8b3f\"/>\
    </radialGradient>\
    <linearGradient id=\"sky\" x1=\"0\" y1=\"0\" x2=\"0\" y2=\"1\">\
      <stop offset=\"0%\" stop-color=\"#cfefff\"/>\
      <stop offset=\"100%\" stop-color=\"#eaf7ff\"/>\
    </linearGradient>\
  </defs>\
  <!-- sky -->\
  <rect x=\"0\" y=\"0\" width=\"512\" height=\"220\" fill=\"url(#sky)\"/>\
  <!-- ground -->\
  <rect x=\"0\" y=\"220\" width=\"512\" height=\"292\" fill=\"url(#grass)\"/>\
  <!-- subtle turf lines -->\
  <path d=\"M0 320 C120 300, 220 360, 330 340 C420 322, 470 335, 512 328\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#1a7a35\" stroke-width=\"3\" opacity=\"0.35\"/>\
  <path d=\"M0 385 C110 362, 240 420, 355 392 C425 375, 475 392, 512 383\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#1a7a35\" stroke-width=\"3\" opacity=\"0.3\"/>\
  <!-- golf hole -->\
  <ellipse cx=\"320\" cy=\"410\" rx=\"26\" ry=\"10\" fill=\"#0c5a26\" opacity=\"0.9\"/>\
  <ellipse cx=\"320\" cy=\"404\" rx=\"18\" ry=\"6\" fill=\"#063a16\" opacity=\"0.95\"/>\
  <circle cx=\"320\" cy=\"408\" r=\"6\" fill=\"#033012\" opacity=\"0.9\"/>\
  <!-- flag pole -->\
  <path d=\"M320 330 L320 400\" stroke=\"#d9d9d9\" stroke-width=\"6\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"/>\
  <path d=\"M320 330 L320 400\" stroke=\"#9c9c9c\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" opacity=\"0.6\"/>\
  <!-- flag -->\
  <path d=\"M320 332 C340 342, 360 338, 378 349 C360 358, 345 360, 320 372 Z\" fill=\"#2c62ff\"/>\
  <path d=\"M320 332 C340 342, 360 338, 378 349\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#1241b8\" stroke-width=\"3\" opacity=\"0.6\"/>\
  <!-- golf ball -->\
  <circle cx=\"235\" cy=\"405\" r=\"18\" fill=\"#ffffff\"/>\
  <circle cx=\"235\" cy=\"405\" r=\"18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#cfcfcf\" stroke-width=\"3\"/>\
  <!-- simple dimples -->\
  <g fill=\"#e6e6e6\" opacity=\"0.9\">\
    <circle cx=\"229\" cy=\"398\" r=\"2\"/>\
    <circle cx=\"241\" cy=\"398\" r=\"2\"/>\
    <circle cx=\"232\" cy=\"409\" r=\"2\"/>\
    <circle cx=\"240\" cy=\"412\" r=\"2\"/>\
    <circle cx=\"228\" cy=\"412\" r=\"1.8\"/>\
    <circle cx=\"235\" cy=\"401\" r=\"1.7\"/>\
  </g>\
  <!-- golf club (behind ball) -->\
  <g transform=\"translate(0,0)\">\
    <path d=\"M120 360 C180 330, 210 330, 245 350\" stroke=\"#6b4b2a\" stroke-width=\"16\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" fill=\"none\"/>\
    <path d=\"M120 360 C180 330, 210 330, 245 350\" stroke=\"#3b2413\" stroke-width=\"4\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" fill=\"none\" opacity=\"0.55\"/>\
    <!-- head -->\
    <path d=\"M245 350 C270 360, 292 382, 300 410 C287 412, 270 405, 260 398 C252 392, 246 382, 245 350 Z\" fill=\"#2f2f2f\"/>\
    <path d=\"M252 376 C268 384, 283 398, 294 410\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"#6d6d6d\" stroke-width=\"4\" opacity=\"0.7\"/>\
    <path d=\"M220 345 L235 355\" stroke=\"#6b4b2a\" stroke-width=\"10\" stroke-linecap=\"round\"/>\
  </g>\
  </svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Creating a golf representation**

I need to create a visual representation of golf without using any letters. It should include a golf ball, a flag hole, and a club. I can keep it simple by using basic shapes: a circle for the ball, a pole with a flag for the hole, and an arc shape for the club. My goal is to return this as an SVG format design. Let's go ahead and put this together!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
